images.title: Tidalites de Bréhec.jpg
images.description: Tidalites of the Port-Lazo Formation on the beach of Anse de Bréhec in Plouha, Côtes-d'Armor department of Brittany in France, outcropping as a stack of red clay-silt laminae and grey-green silt-sand laminae, each a few millimetres in thickness. The red laminae contain ferric iron oxides characteristic of a well-oxygenated and therefore agitated depositional environment. The green laminae contain ferrous iron oxides characteristic of a chemically-reducing depositional environment with calmer water.

images.title: Bréhec - pano.jpg
images.description: Panoramic view of the port and beach of Bréhec, in Brittany. The coast on the left is on Plouézec. Plouha's territory starts with the beach, including the harbour itself.

images.title: Conglomerate Brehec Brittany France.jpg
images.description: Conglomerate of the Port-Lazo Formation of Ordovician age at Brehec in Brittany, France. Most of the pebbles in the rock are made of quartz and quartzite. The photo shows an area about 1 metre across.
